title: campanula medium
intro: Campanula medium, commonly known as Canterbury bells, is a flowering plant species in the family Campanulaceae. It has been used in herbal medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and easing digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Campanula medium include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
